assignments.twist: Q-learning value iteration (discounted return, max over price actions) used to learn a personalized dynamic pricing policy
Bertrand price-competition among charging stations with a Nash-stable partition constraint on self-interested users' station choices

core_model_summary: A reinforcement-learning dynamic pricing policy in which a charging station maximizes discounted revenue via Q-learning while self-interested users choose stations according to a game-theoretic (Bertrand/Nash-stable) equilibrium selection model.
